ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING , REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-01-2015 Continued To Sep-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun & Dave Parnall for and with James Bennett. Michael Beuselinck for Patricia Gridley. Adrian Sawyer & Jeff Baumann for and with Michael Gridley and for Robert Gridley.

Ms. Calhoun stated that they are attempting to gain certainty of costs and that Michael Gridley is asking for attorney's fees that should not be approved. The Court stated that provisional judge's opinions are in this case and that it does not believe it would be definitive as to what could be used. Mr. Sawyer stated his agreement with the court, stated that objections were just received yesterday, that the objections raise issues with the accountings, that they need a result that everyone can live with, that they do not see the benefits of addressing the merits of the accounting and that it may be more productive to have rulings on the motions and then a chance to mediate. Ms. Calhoun stated that they would like a declaration on the unaddressed fees supported by invoices, that they are tying up loose ends with the ENGIT matter, that per the 5/7/13 order the attorney's fees were to be shown as a debt to be repaid to the trust and that the trustees 4/24/13 motion for fees was only tentatively granted and that there has been no final order. The parties agreed that the accounting petition should be continued until after mediation. The accounting petition was continued to September 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for instructions to trustee to transfer funds (currently set for 7/6/15) is continued to this date as well.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Saephan. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Trustee Michael Gridley'S Petition For Extraordinary Fees
Off Calendar May-01-2015 Advanced From May-20-15 To May-18-15 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Trustee Michael Gridley'S Petition For Extraordinary Fees
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Wagstaffe & Jeff Baumann for and with Michael Gridley. Emilie Calhoun & Dave Parnall for James Bennett. Michael Beuselinck (via court call) for (Beneficiary) Patricia Gridley.

Mr. Wagstaffe presented oral argument stating that it would have been difficult for Mr. Gridley to anticipate these circumstances, how P.C. 15680 & 15686 apply here, that extraordinary fees do not re-write the contract, spoke of extraordinary circumstances calling for equitable relief, fee increases on fixed compensation, reiterated that Justice Lowe and Judge Wiss ordered that these matters be considered at the end and that it is nonsensical. Ms. Calhoun stated that there are no new facts being presented here, that argument should have been made prior to the ruling, that the 1% set fee was stipulated to by all of the beneficiaries and that there are no new facts just new argument. Mr. Parnall stated that a lot of arguments are equitable arguments, that something could have been done in 2003, that the burden is on the trustee and his attorneys to show why arguments were not made in an earlier motion and that P.C. 15680 is crystal clear on its face. Mr. Beuselinck stated that his review of the original moving papers found no mention of 15680, that the language in the statute is clear, that even if they are considering new facts equitable relief is not provided for, that they are asking for the trust contract to be re-written and that why the information was not brought previously is not a proper basis for seeking fees. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that a declaration has been submitted, that they had been told to wait until the end and that tomorrow is the last day to file an appeal. The Court stated that it would get its order out as soon as possible. The Motion for Reconsideration of order denying trustee Michael Gridley's Petition for Extraordinary Fees was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Motion For Attorneys' Fees (Motion Previously Filed On July 9, 2013)
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING , REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-01-2015 Continued To Jun-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Wagstaffe & Jeff Baumann for and with Michael Gridley. Emilie Calhoun & David Parnall for and with James Bennett. G. Scott Haislet (via court call) for Patricia Gridley.

The Court stated that we are in the final hoops of the case and asked if there was an advantage to doing this piece-meal. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that he was not sure there was and referred to a previous order stating "all will be subject to off-sets in the end", that it can all be done globally and stated that the trust is not under court supervision. Ms. Calhoun stated that they have a lack of trust in the trustee, that there are inconsistencies and lack of information, fees were not reimbursed to the trust, that there was a significant omission of $50,000.00, that they hired a professional fiduciary to check the accounting and find that it does not show payment to the trust by Michael Gridley, cited 1060 indicating that accountings must comply with the code whether under court supervision or not and requests that the court bring this trust under court supervision. Mr. Haislet reported that he filed a 17200 petition yesterday, that fees were ordered to be paid 10 years ago, that the trust accounting needs to be updated through the end of 2013 and that Patricia and Christine could have been paid out the $800,000.00 that was paid into the trust. Ms. Calhoun expressed her concerns regarding the repayment to the trust by Michael per the 5/7/13 order, the request for trustee fees for Mr. Nicora & Mr. Davidian, the repayment to the trust of excess of what was paid to Kerr & Wagstaffe and that attorney fees by decreased or trustee asked to parse out what the fees and invoices were for. Mr. Haislet stated that trust funds should not be used on a personal judgment, that the 9th accounting states that it is a final accounting but there is no proposed distribution and that there are other matters coming up. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that they have submitted previous accountings in the same format and that they were approved. The Court asked about the possibility of mediation, asked what are the pieces to mediate and asked what the distribution time frame would be. Mr. Wagstaffe reported that he has a motion for attorney's fees as well as a motion for reconsideration pending which he needs an earlier date for to be able to appeal if necessary. Ms. Calhoun reported that she had met with Mr. Wagstaffe, that she has concerns regarding the ability to reach an agreement, asked why the offsets were not done previously and that the 7th & 8th accountings were approved after hearings on the objections. Mr. Haislet stated that they would like to settle the case, that there needs to be a thorough, complete & up to date accounting with full disclosure in a genuine and honest way and that he feels this will continue until the trust is diminished. The Court stated the following: the motion for attorney's fees will be taken under submission as of May 6, 2015 and the parties are to submit respective proposed orders by that date; the motion for reconsideration of order denying, etc., currently scheduled for May 20, 2015 will be advanced to May 18, 2015 at 2:00 p.m. in dept. 204; the accounting petition was continued to June 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for an amended accounting petition to be filed with a conception of the proposed distribution; the parties are to schedule mediation with Judge Wiss before July 31, 2015 and are instructed to contact the clerk for that department and a status conference is set for June 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Handa. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Continued To Feb-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PES-92-259147 Estate of Elsie N. Gridley and PES-04-286102 Estate of Arnold Gridley.
Appearances: James Wagstaffe for and with (trustee) Michael Gridley. G. Scott Haislet for and with (beneficiary) Patricia Gridley. Emilie Calhoun & Dave Parnall for and with (special administrator of Christine Bennett's estate) James Bennett.

Mr. Wagstaffe provided case history/background, referred to prior Judicial Officers decisions/opinions, prior settlement agreements and prior court orders. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to trustees fees, attorney fees, extraordinary fees for both trustee and attorney, the 9th accounting not balancing and whether or not it is in proper format, lack of documentation to support time entries, whether or not the court has jurisdiction over the trust, whether the accounting should include all reimbursements, whether the judgment belongs to the trust/should be a trust asset, etc. The parties all agree that a further accounting will be needed. Mediation was discussed. Ms. Calhoun provided Mr. Wagstaffe with an oral list of items she is requesting. Mr. Wagstaffe agreed to provide counsel with information regarding valuation of the Vacaville property. The petition for trustees extraordinary fees and the motion for offset were continued to February 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to submit additional briefs by February 18, 2015. Said matters will be under submission on that date. The accounting petition and the motion for attorney's fees were continued to March 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Wagstaffe to provide additional information that may be helpful.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clark. (Not Reported)
